```

date::Date = Date(arg, "yyyymmdd")
```

Which produces the following error.

```
ERROR: LoadError: ArgumentError: Month: 41 out of range (1:12)
```

Apologies if this is a duplicate or non issue. I did have a look to see if I could find the reason why this is happening, but it looked like it might be a more difficult issue to track down than I had initially thought.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56301/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56301/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2431561232","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56301#issuecomment-2431561232","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56301","id":2431561232,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6Q7q4Q","user":{"login":"edward-bestx","id":183390225,"node_id":"U_kgDOCu5QEQ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/183390225?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/edward-bestx","html_url":"https://github.com/edward-bestx","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edward-bestx/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edward-bestx/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edward-bestx/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edward-bestx/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edward-bestx/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edward-bestx/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edward-bestx/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edward-bestx/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/edward-bestx/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-23T09:51:55Z","updated_at":"2024-10-23T09:51:55Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> Did you mean to write `"20241022"`?

No, this is what I think is a bug.

The date `200241022` should be interpreted as year=20024 October 22nd. It isn't interpreted as this - the month is 41 instead of 10.

I realize that it is unlikely that someone would want to parse a date for the year 20024, however such a year should be a valid input.

I thought it might be possible that this indicates a bug somewhere else in the Dates library.

**What behavior would I expect to see?**

- Since the datetime format was specified as "yyyymmdd" there should perhaps be a warning that the number of digits for the year is 5 instead of 4.
- Alternatively, maybe an exception should be thrown.
- Something to consider: Should the format have been "yyyyymmdd" (with 5x "y") ? Probably not - how would a year like 2024 be interpreted in such cases? Would you have to write 02024 ? Maybe that's the right way.
- How does it behave for this date: 9990101 (1st January year 999) when the datetime format is "yyyymmdd" ? There is a digit missing for the year.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2431561232/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

That doesn't make any sense, because the user didn't pass `111` as the number of days.

If you think it's not significant enough of an issue, as I already said, please feel free to close this issue.

If we align both strings vertically, the issue becomes obvious:

```
 "200211111"
"yyyymmdd"
```

It could be aligned either way. For numerical things, which something like a date is, we read them right to left, not left to right.

For example the number `1234567`. You know this is "about 1 million". The reason you know it is "about 1 million" is because you read it right to left. You count the digits, effectively, reading them as groups of 3.
